title: Red Hat Security Advisory: kernel-rt security update
overview:
An update for kernel-rt is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

full_text:
Red Hat Security Advisory: kernel-rt security update

An update for kernel-rt is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

An update for kernel-rt is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

Updated Packages:
kernel-rt-4.18.0-553.84.1.rt7.425.el8_10.src.rpm
kernel-rt-4.18.0-553.84.1.rt7.425.el8_10.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-core-4.18.0-553.84.1.rt7.425.el8_10.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-debug-4.18.0-553.84.1.rt7.425.el8_10.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-debug-core-4.18.0-553.84.1.rt7.425.el8_10.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-debug-debuginfo-4.18.0-553.84.1.rt7.425.el8_10.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-debug-devel-4.18.0-553.84.1.rt7.425.el8_10.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-debug-modules-4.18.0-553.84.1.rt7.425.el8_10.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-debug-modules-extra-4.18.0-553.84.1.rt7.425.el8_10.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-debuginfo-4.18.0-553.84.1.rt7.425.el8_10.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-debuginfo-common-x86_64-4.18.0-553.84.1.rt7.425.el8_10.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-devel-4.18.0-553.84.1.rt7.425.el8_10.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-modules-4.18.0-553.84.1.rt7.425.el8_10.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-modules-extra-4.18.0-553.84.1.rt7.425.el8_10.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-debug-kvm-4.18.0-553.84.1.rt7.425.el8_10.x86_64.rpm
kernel-rt-kvm-4.18.0-553.84.1.rt7.425.el8_10.x86_64.rpm

CVE-2025-39718: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

vsock/virtio: Validate length in packet header before skb_put()

When receiving a vsock packet in the guest, only the virtqueue buffer
size is validated prior to virtio_vsock_skb_rx_put(). Unfortunately,
virtio_vsock_skb_rx_put() uses the length from the packet header as the
length argument to skb_put(), potentially resulting in SKB overflow if
the host has gone wonky.

Validate the length as advertised by the packet header before calling
virtio_vsock_skb_rx_put().
